name: Maguindanaon

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Maguindanao (magu1243).

notes: Skeleton. Maguindanaon is a Danao (Greater Central Philippine) Austronesian language of Mindanao, written in a Latin alphabet. The grapheme map covers the consonant inventory plus the digraph ng for the velar nasal and the four-vowel system, with the letter e mapped to schwa (the historical pepet vowel). Vowels i and u have lower allophones ([e]/[o]) not distinguished in spelling. The phonemic glottal stop is not consistently written and is left unencoded, as are stress and any length. Arabic-loan consonants beyond the native set are omitted from this skeleton.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Maguindanao (magu1243)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
